title: Herbert On The Bombing Of Caen
rights: public domain (presumed; pre-1963 broadcast, no rights statement on archive.org item)
transcript: Now, gray and purple smoke is filling up in the kind area. The results of this visual bombing attack are really terrific. Hundreds upon hundreds of pounds of bombs are raining down in the stricken town, and still the bombers come pouring in. There seems to be no end to them. This is an attack in a big way. The enemy is throwing up everything he has in the book, but he seems not able to get in any hits on our plate. Thoughts of near misses, but no telling shots. One bomber passing overhead has lost part of his tail assembly. The whole of the pilot is lower than the other planes and his wife. He's keeping her straight and steady on a homework court. The whole target area is receiving massive smoke. In the growing darkness, I can see the first dull red outline of the fire that is apparently raging in the cat. It'll be a big bonfire soon. And you can look for those words. Big fires reflect burning into Mars communicate. Pilotless planes could never do a job like this, I'll bet. And now smoke is spreading over time. Great clouds of it mushrooming upwards and the flames are beginning to show an angry red color.
